solution: 
```cpp
class Solution {
public:
    string lastSubstring(string s) {
        int n = s.size();
        int maxIndex = 0;
        for (int i = 1; i < n; ++i) {
            int j = 0;
            while (i + j < n && s[maxIndex + j] == s[i + j]) {
                ++j;
            }
            if (i + j < n && s[maxIndex + j] < s[i + j]) {
                maxIndex = i;
            }
            i += j;
        }
        return s.substr(maxIndex);
    }
};
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(n)
space complexity: O(1)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: The algorithm efficiently finds the last substring in lexicographical order by comparing characters and skipping unnecessary comparisons using the result of previous comparisons. It ensures linear time complexity by avoiding redundant checks.
